message:
fix(tutte_polynomial): match nx signature, retain numeric extension (br-tuttesig)

nx.tutte_polynomial(G) returns a symbolic sympy polynomial in x, y.
fnx's wrapper required ``(G, x, y)`` positional args and evaluated the
polynomial at that point, so drop-in calls like ``nx.tutte_polynomial(G)``
raised ``TypeError: missing 2 required positional arguments``.

Fix: default ``x`` and ``y`` to None. When both None, delegate to nx
(returns sympy polynomial; raises ModuleNotFoundError if sympy missing,
matching nx's behaviour). When both provided, keep fnx's numeric
deletion-contraction evaluator as a sympy-free extension. Partial
args (only one of x, y) raise TypeError with a clear message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
